constraints.text: Raw sessions cannot be committed as source artifacts for the Agent Memory Graph.
The protocol cannot export secrets or sensitive local data into repo-local dashboard artifacts.

decisions.text: The Agent Memory Graph uses a global memory root outside the repo while exporting read-only projections back into repo-local artifacts.
The agent-graph CLI provides bootstrap, init-repo, validate, archive-session, and export as the portable protocol surface.
Raw sessions are source material and explicit last-resort context rather than the default input layer.

requirements.text: Every adopting repository must commit a repo-local .agent/context.json manifest that points exports into deterministic artifacts paths.
archive-session ingests curated compiled-session JSON artifacts rather than raw session dumps.

summary: The global Agent Memory Graph protocol introduced a shared memory root, a repo-scoped .agent/context.json manifest, agent-graph bootstrap/init-repo/validate/archive-session/export commands, and a rule that raw sessions stay source material and last-resort context.
